blocks.body: Past Perfect Continuous: Forms and Use — had been + V-ing for a process that was continuing up to another past action or past moment.

{"level":"A2–B1","intro":"In Past Perfect Continuous, we use had been + V-ing when we want to show an action that started earlier and was still continuing up to another past event or up to a past moment.","rules":[{"label":"had been + V-ing","color":"emerald","text":"This is the basic affirmative form for all subjects.","example":"I had been working. / They had been waiting."},{"label":"Before another past event","color":"blue","text":"The tense shows a process that was already continuing before another event in the past.","example":"She had been studying before the exam started."},{"label":"Focus on duration","color":"amber","text":"It often matters not only that something happened, but also how long the process lasted.","example":"We had been driving for three hours before we stopped."}]}

{"title":"1. Basic formula","intro":"Remember how the Past Perfect Continuous form is built.","items":[{"label":"I / You / We / They","title":"had been + V-ing","subtitle":"I had been working. / They had been waiting."},{"label":"He / She / It","title":"had been + V-ing","subtitle":"She had been studying. / It had been raining."},{"label":"Spelling","title":"work → working, live → living, run → running","subtitle":"After been, use the verb with -ing."},{"label":"Meaning","title":"process / duration before then","subtitle":"The action was already continuing before another past action or past moment."}]}

{"title":"2. When we use Past Perfect Continuous","sections":[{"label":"Before another event","color":"emerald","description":"Show a process that was continuing before another event in the past.","examples":[{"en":"They had been waiting for an hour before the doors opened.","ua":"The waiting was already in progress before the next event."},{"en":"I had been reading when the lights went out.","ua":"Reading was the background process."}]},{"label":"Up to a past moment","color":"blue","description":"Talk about duration that led up to a specific moment in the past.","examples":[{"en":"By 5 p.m., she had been working for six hours.","ua":"The duration reached that point in the past."},{"en":"We had been driving since morning before we stopped.","ua":"The action continued up to that later moment."}]},{"label":"Reason in the past","color":"amber","description":"This tense often explains a past state or result through an earlier process.","examples":[{"en":"He was tired because he had been running.","ua":"The earlier process explains the later state."},{"en":"Her eyes were red because she had been crying.","ua":"The past result comes from the earlier process."}]},{"label":"Background in a story","color":"violet","description":"The tense helps give background before another action in a narrative.","examples":[{"en":"They had been talking quietly before the teacher came in.","ua":"The action forms the background in the story."},{"en":"I had been looking for that file when my manager called.","ua":"The process was already happening at that time."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Quick form check","intro":"See how had been works with V-ing.","rows":[{"en":"I had been waiting for an hour before the train arrived.","ua":"had been waiting","note":"I + had been + V-ing"},{"en":"She had been studying all evening before the test.","ua":"had been studying","note":"she + had been + V-ing"},{"en":"They had been living there for years before they moved.","ua":"had been living","note":"they + had been + V-ing"},{"en":"The ground was wet because it had been raining.","ua":"had been raining","note":"had been + V-ing for a past cause"}],"warning":"Do not say She had been work or I had been waited. After had been, you need V-ing."}

{"title":"4. Short summary","items":["Past Perfect Continuous = had been + V-ing.","The form had been is the same for all subjects.","After had been, use the verb with -ing.","This tense highlights a process or duration before another past action or past moment."]}
